content:
## When Provisional Credit Applies
Provisional credit is available only when the dispute meets the full eligibility set: can_file, written_statement_provided, account_status_is_open, account_has_no_holds, category_allows_provisional_credit, pin_not_shared_voluntarily, not_new_account_card_not_present_combo. Treat the list as cumulative; satisfying only part of it is not enough to approve the credit. If any required condition is missing from the customer record or dispute intake, do not quote an approval outcome until the missing fact is confirmed.

## Timing Customers Can Be Quoted
For eligible standard cases, the provisional credit due window is 10 business days. For eligible new-account cases, use 20 business days instead. When speaking with a customer, describe the applicable window after confirming whether the account falls under the standard or new-account timing rule.

## Common Denial Points
A case does not qualify when the required eligibility set is not fully met. Common blockers are captured inside can_file, written_statement_provided, account_status_is_open, account_has_no_holds, category_allows_provisional_credit, pin_not_shared_voluntarily, not_new_account_card_not_present_combo, including filing status, written statement status, account condition, category eligibility, PIN-sharing facts, and the new-account/card-not-present combination rule. See "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy" for the formal policy page and "Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements" for intake handling.
title: Debit Provisional Credit FAQ
